projects.name: Dirtbagz
projects.description: Build a team of junkyard robots and play NES-style baseball. 9-robot roster with 5-part customization, 7 stat attributes, full pitching/batting/fielding mechanics.
projects.notes: Biggest gaps: audio (0%), visual polish (5%). MVP ~15% away. Game loop stability needs testing. 4.1MB stadium PNG needs compression.

runGame.summary: The run.game connector software is installed and the game knows how to pause and resume when you switch apps. But it's missing the config file needed to publish — you'd need to run a setup command to create that. It also doesn't save progress to the cloud yet, and has no haptic feedback or ads set up.
runGame.readiness: Almost ready. Needs config file created, then it can be published.

projects.name: Ultraviolet
projects.description: 3D endless runner built from scratch with Three.js. Custom world curvature shaders, object pooling, particle system, collision detection with 4 major iterations of refinement.
projects.notes: Near-complete. Has detailed technical deep-dive doc (FOR-UltraViolette.md). Custom rendering engine, no third-party game framework.

runGame.summary: Fully deployed and live on run.game. Saves your high scores to the cloud, vibrates your phone on impacts, and properly pauses when you switch apps. The only thing not set up is ads (for making money from the game).
runGame.readiness: Live and playable on run.game right now.

projects.name: RetroStrikers
projects.description: 2D retro arcade soccer with dynamic AI (chaser, zone defense, fleeing), intelligent teammate AI, skill-based controls, player armor system, goalkeeper deflection mechanics.
projects.notes: Feature-complete and polished. Has .image-style.json configured. Multiple AI refinements documented in README.

runGame.summary: This is a plain web game with no run.game integration at all. It was built as a standalone browser game using basic HTML and JavaScript. To put it on run.game, you'd need to install the connector software, add mobile touch controls (it currently only uses keyboard), and set it up for phone screens.
runGame.readiness: Needs full run.game setup from scratch.

projects.name: Sugar Rush Delivery
projects.description: Hyper-casual runner-shooter inspired by Last War/Kingshot. Auto-forward movement with lane-swiping, auto-fire, wave-based survival, multiplier gate mechanic.
projects.notes: Phase 1C based on QA test report. Has ART_ASSET_LIST.md. Needs asset generation and gameplay refinement.

runGame.summary: Uses the newest version of the run.game connector software (called 'Rundot Game SDK' v5.0.4 — newer than what most other projects use). The game knows how to pause and resume properly. But it still needs a config file to publish, and doesn't save progress or have ads yet.
runGame.readiness: Almost ready. Needs config file created, then it can be published.

projects.name: CoralQuest
projects.description: Non-jumping puzzle exploration (Captain Toad inspired). Isometric camera rotation, AI-powered narrator with LLM-driven micro-stories, grid-based movement, collectible system.
projects.notes: Playable prototype. Core mechanics ready. Needs player character, collision detection, AI narrator integration. Has .image-style.json (cottagecore watercolor).

runGame.summary: This is a plain web game with no run.game integration. It's built with basic HTML and JavaScript. To put it on run.game, you'd need to install the connector software, add mobile-friendly controls, and set it up for phone screens.
runGame.readiness: Needs full run.game setup from scratch.

projects.name: Gateaux
projects.description: Educational cake-shop game for learning French and Spanish. Display case system with aging mechanics, lesson management, customer service minigames, NPC audio.
projects.notes: Pre-Alpha. Visual assets are critical path (0% complete, 110+ images needed). Uses vibeforge CLI. 13-week estimated timeline to MVP.

runGame.summary: Deployed and has a live page on run.game. Saves your game progress to the cloud, vibrates on interactions, and pauses properly. The SDK version is set to 'any available' (wildcard) instead of a specific version — this means it always grabs whatever is newest, which could occasionally cause surprises. No ads set up yet.
runGame.readiness: Live on run.game. Consider locking SDK to a specific version.

projects.name: Forest of Fools
projects.description: Cooperative narrative game set in Midsummer Night's Dream. 10-12 minute runs, 3 player roles with action card system, AI story generation, communication/conflict mechanics.
projects.notes: Design complete (GDD.md, ART_DESIGN.md), 0% development. Has 10-milestone plan in Design/MILESTONES.md. Story-first design philosophy. Has generated concept art.

runGame.summary: This project only has design documents and concept art so far — no actual game code exists yet. run.game integration will happen when development begins.
runGame.readiness: Not applicable yet — no game code to deploy.

projects.name: BizzyDad
projects.description: Family organizer with Claude-powered assistants. Dashboard with assistant tiles, chat UI, file operations with audit logging, propose/apply workflow for edits.
projects.notes: Monorepo: React frontend + Node.js backend. Uses Supabase DB and Anthropic SDK for AI features. Not a game — it's a productivity app.

runGame.summary: This is not a game — it's a family organizer app powered by AI. It's designed to run as a regular web app (hosted on Vercel), not on the run.game platform. run.game is for games only.
runGame.readiness: Not applicable — this is a web app, not a game.

projects.name: Hello Whirled
projects.description: Procedurally generated 3D planets with flora, theme system, tab-based navigation. Previously published on run.game. Most complete run.game SDK integration of any project.
projects.notes: Development complete. Previously published on run.game but currently having deployment issues. Most complete run.game integration — uses every SDK feature. Good reference for adding run.game to other projects.

runGame.summary: This is your most complete run.game integration — it uses every feature the platform offers: saving data to the cloud, phone vibrations, ads (both the kind that pop up and the kind players choose to watch for rewards), device detection, and popup messages. It just needs a config file created to publish. This is a great reference when adding run.game features to other projects.
runGame.readiness: Fully integrated. Just needs config file to publish.

projects.name: Agincort
projects.description: Early-stage 3D game project. Has Three.js foundation with procedural gradient sky, particle system, animation utilities with easing, raycasting, mobile responsive (9:16 frame on desktop).
projects.notes: Very early stage — no GDD, no milestone plan, no development work done. Has Three.js starter code with lighting, shadows, particle system, raycasting. Basic run.game integration.

runGame.summary: This is a starter template for building 3D games. It has the run.game connector software installed with basic logging, but most features (saving data, phone vibrations, pausing when you switch apps) haven't been wired up yet. That's expected — it's a template you'd build on top of.
runGame.readiness: Template — meant to be copied and built upon.
